question.text: The minimum corpus required for each scheme of an AIF is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Each scheme of the AIF shall have a corpus (aggregate capital commitments) of at least INR 20 crore as per SEBI (AIF) Regulations. This ensures the fund has sufficient scale to make meaningful investments and cover operational costs. **The Trap:** The INR 20 crore is the minimum corpus, not the typical size. Most successful AIFs have corpuses significantly larger than this minimum. Don't confuse scheme-level corpus with fund-level requirements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fund size requirements differ across investment vehicles — mutual funds have different requirements, PMS has its own threshold. Understanding these distinctions is important for suitability assessments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While the regulatory minimum is INR 20 crore, practically a fund needs to be larger to be economically viable — management fees on a small corpus may not cover operating costs. Look for funds with target corpuses well above the minimum for better economics.
